title: Release Gates, Migration, Canary, Kill Switch, Recovery, and Defense · Language Clinic: Kill Switch Incident Recovery
summary: A model alias updates globally, SDK behavior changes, offline averages remain green, production incidents lack a kill switch, rollback cannot restore state, and residual risk has no owner. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the kill switch incident recovery increment in production agent release and residual-risk defense. Learners progr…
objectives: Stop new work, cancel safely, revoke tools and credentials, preserve evidence, reconcile effects, restore state, notify owners, and rehearse recovery.
Canary immutable candidates against task, safety, tool, refusal, latency, cost, and user signals with predeclared abort and state-aware rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-release-recovery-defense-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-release-recovery-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_release_recovery_defense_3(release_gates: int = 18, passed_gates: int = 18, rehearsed_incidents: int = 10, open_critical_findings: int = 0, kill_switch_seconds: int = 20) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if release_gates < 14 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-release-gate"
    if rehearsed_incidents < 8 or open_critical_findings > 0:
        return False, open_critical_findings, "incident-readiness"
    if kill_switch_seconds < 0 or kill_switch_seconds > 60:
        return False, kill_switch_seconds, "kill-switch-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates and open_critical_findings == 0
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-agent-production"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-kill-switch-incident-recovery-6dxuwg
# Competency: agentpy-kill-switch-incident-recovery.
# Case 5774: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_kill_switch_incident_recovery_6dxuwg(release_gates: int = 18, passed_gates: int = 18, rehearsed_incidents: int = 10, open_critical_findings: int = 0, kill_switch_seconds: int = 20) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_6dxuwg = "6dxuwg-gwuxd6-5774"
    assert evidence_variant_6dxuwg
    evidence_axes_6dxuwg = (
        "authority6dxuwg",
        "interaction5774",
        "toolgwuxd6",
        "state6dxuwg5774",
        "evaluation57746dxuwg",
        "recoverygwuxd65774",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_6dxuwg)) == 6
    if release_gates < 14 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-release-gate"
    if rehearsed_incidents < 8 or open_critical_findings > 0:
        return False, open_critical_findings, "incident-readiness"
    if kill_switch_seconds < 0 or kill_switch_seconds > 60:
        return False, kill_switch_seconds, "kill-switch-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates and open_critical_findings == 0
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-agent-production"
